step_by_step_thinking:
For an 11-year-old boy complaining of spacing between his maxillary incisors, the appropriate treatment would involve addressing orthodontic concerns rather than focusing solely on primary teeth, since he now has permanent teeth. Given the age and complaint about space development, fixed appliances like those attached to adjacent teeth can help correct the alignment without needing extractions or removable devices typically seen in younger children who still have mixed dentitions.
\boxed{B} Based on the information given, option B - "Fixed appliance" seems most suitable for treating spacing issues in a preadolescent individual with fully erupted permanent maxillary incisors. A fixed appliance allows for precise correction of misalignment through attachments bonded to existing teeth, making it ideal for this case compared to other options.
